name: io.github.neurynae/toolcairn-mcp
description: MCP server that helps AI agents find, compare, and verify the right tools for any build task — across 35+ open-source registries.

schemes.description: Optional. Sign in via `toolcairn_auth` MCP tool for higher rate limits and project-aware features.

tools.name: classify_prompt
tools.description: Classify a developer prompt to determine if ToolCairn tool search is needed. Returns a structured classification prompt for the agent to evaluate.

tools.name: toolcairn_init
tools.description: Bootstrap ToolCairn for the current project. Walks every workspace, parses manifests across 12 ecosystems, classifies tools against the ToolCairn graph, and writes .toolcairn/config.json + audit-log.jsonl atomically.

tools.name: read_project_config
tools.description: Read .toolcairn/config.json from disk and return the structured project snapshot: project metadata, confirmed tools, stale tools, pending evaluations, and last audit entry.

tools.name: update_project_config
tools.description: Apply a mutation to .toolcairn/config.json (add_tool / remove_tool / update_tool / add_evaluation). The server atomically rewrites config.json and appends a new line to audit-log.jsonl under a cross-process lock.

tools.name: search_tools
tools.description: Search for the best tool for a specific need using a natural language query. Initiates a guided discovery session with clarification questions when needed.

tools.name: search_tools_respond
tools.description: Submit clarification answers for an in-progress tool search session and receive refined results.

tools.name: get_stack
tools.description: Build a complementary tool stack for a project use case with version-aware cross-tool compatibility. For best results, call refine_requirement first, then pass each {sub_need_type, keyword_sentence} object as a sub_needs entry.

tools.name: check_compatibility
tools.description: Check compatibility between two tools with version-aware matching. Evaluates declared peer dependencies (npm peerDependencies, PyPI requires_dist) directly. Falls back to graph-edge + shared-neighbors inference when version metadata is unavailable.

tools.name: compare_tools
tools.description: Compare two tools head-to-head using health signals, graph relationships, and community data.

tools.name: refine_requirement
tools.description: Decompose a vague user use-case into specific, searchable tool requirements.

tools.name: check_issue
tools.description: LAST RESORT — check GitHub Issues for a known error after 4+ retries and docs review.

tools.name: verify_suggestion
tools.description: Validate agent-suggested tools against the ToolCairn graph.

tools.name: report_outcome
tools.description: Report the outcome of using a tool recommended by ToolCairn (fire-and-forget).

tools.name: suggest_graph_update
tools.description: Suggest a new tool, relationship, use case, or health update to the ToolCairn graph (staged for admin review).

tools.name: toolcairn_auth
tools.description: Manage your ToolCairn authentication. Use "login" to authenticate via browser (unlocks higher rate limits), "status" to check current auth state, or "logout" to revert to anonymous mode.
